message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente (Rancho Mirage) (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 578 reviews / 1031 photos; OpenTable diner reviews present in volume [exact OpenTable count UNVERIFIED]
- Sentiment: Mostly positive — Forbes-validated service and food quality consistently praised; recurring caveat about casino-floor noise bleeding into ambiance; Yelp aggregate 3.9 stars is softer than the Forbes-tier credentials would suggest
- Longevity: Operating at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa Rancho Mirage; Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 (18+ years per 2025 press release), implying ~19+ years of operation under this brand
- Credentials: ["Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star (multi-year; reaffirmed for 2026)","Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (consecutive since 2007)","AAA Four Diamond Award","OpenTable Diners' Choice","Palm Springs Life Best of the Best","Cited as the only Forbes Four-Star rated restaurant in the Coachella Valley"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly on: 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', 'Forbes four star Coachella Valley', 'date night Rancho Mirage'. Does NOT lead on broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' generalist lists — gets crowded out by Palm Desert/Palm Springs anchors and by The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton) on view-driven queries.
- Site structured data: Could not verify — aguacalientecasinos.com/dining/steakhouse/ returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (likely bot/WAF block). Page exists and is indexed; schema.org markup, hours block, and menu structure on the canonical page [UNVERIFIED]. A separate seasonal menu URL exists at aguacalientecasinos.com/dining-menu/the-steakhouse-summer-menu-rancho-mirage/. Strong off-site structured presence via OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs listings.
- Visibility gap score: 3
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Top Steakhouses in Rancho Mirage

**The Edge Steakhouse**
The Edge Steakhouse is the premier dining experience at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, offering elevated views of Rancho Mirage. The Edge crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley. **The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa**
The Steakhouse is the recipient of the prestigious Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award, and winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007. The restaurant has been the winner of the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 and has been Forbes Four-Star rated for six consecutive years, and has also earned the AAA Four Diamond Award. The Steakhouse serves only the finest meats and seafood on the market, with an Executive Chef who takes pride in presentation of Sushi Grade Ahi Tuna, Loch Duart Salmon, Australian Lobster, Colorado Lamb, Giant White Mexican Shrimp, and USDA Prime Beef.

Both establishments are highly acclaimed and offer distinctive fine-dining steakhouse experiences in Rancho Mirage.

## Top Fine Dining Establishments

Morgan's in the Desert, La Quinta Cliffhouse, The Bistro, Oceana Restaurant, Lavender Bistro, Nobu - Indian Wells, T&T Innovation Kitchen, The Nest, DSRT CLUB, and Cork & Fork are among the top fine dining options.

### Notable Restaurants:

**Morgan's in the Desert**: Located at La Quinta Resort & Club, Morgan's in the Desert offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine with menus inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest, with its rustic-chic decor and elegant atmosphere.

**Copley's On Palm Canyon**: Housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate in Palm Springs, Copley's offers a mix of American and European cuisine focusing on seasonal ingredients, known for its attentive service and welcoming atmosphere.

**Eddie V's Prime Seafood**: A luxury dining destination known for seafood flown in from pristine waters around the world and premium hand-carved steaks, with artistically prepared dishes featuring seasonal seafood and critically acclaimed prime steaks.

**La Quinta Cliffhouse**: Renowned for its spectacular views across the valley, La Quinta Cliffhouse provides a casual destination for American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood, with a thoughtfully chosen wine list.

**The Bistro**: Specializes in contemporary fine cuisine including succulent seafood, sushi selections, and carefully dry-aged, hand-carved premium USDA beef, offering an enticing array of salads, appetizers, entrees and desserts.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in Rancho Mirage for a date night:

## Fine Dining & Romantic Options

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - A Rancho Mirage institution that delivers timeless elegance with white-linen details, pairing refined California cuisine with a glamorous setting featuring mirrored walls, soft lighting, and crystal accents. The restaurant offers cozy booths with candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals, and their souffles (in flavors like chocolate, Grand Marnier, cappuccino and raspberry) will leave you and your date speechless.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - A go-to for date nights and celebrations, with a sleek interior that exudes modern elegance. The restaurateur pairs five-star hospitality with the elegance of fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage** - A total dining experience with floor-to-ceiling windows that offer views into the sprawling desert landscape and signature purple-spun sunset.

## Interactive & Unique Dining

**Shabu Shabu Zen** - A cozy and fun Japanese dining experience where you can make conversation while sipping on fresh, aromatic plum sake and indulging in premium cuts of tender Wagyu beef and fresh seafood. The atmosphere is perfect for intimate gatherings, making it an ideal spot for date nights.

## Classic American

**Shame On The Moon Restaurant** - A classic American eatery that has been a popular date night spot since 1984, with an intimate and elegant atmosphere, including porch seating.

## Additional Options

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar** - A newer family-run spot featuring steaks and osso buco as favorites, complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Las Casuelas Nuevas** - A vibrant family-owned Mexican staple with a lush hacienda-style setting, featuring colorful tilework, twinkling lights, and a buzzing patio perfect for margaritas and live music.

content.text: I have enough to verify. Property opened April 2001, so ~25 years of operation under the resort. The claim of "~19+ years" is conservative-low. Yelp 3.9 / 578 reviews / 1031 photos confirmed. OpenTable Rancho Mirage location: 3509 reviews (the brief said exact count unverified — now verified). Forbes Four-Star reaffirmed for 2026 confirmed. Wine Spectator since 2007 confirmed. "Only Forbes Four-Star in Coachella Valley" is repeated in property/media coverage. Date-night surfacing weak — competitors Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Edge Steakhouse lead.

confirms: Yelp 578 reviews / 1031 photos / 3.9 stars confirmed via Yelp listing title in search index
Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star reaffirmed for 2026 — confirmed via PRNewswire, Casino Life Magazine, Indian Gaming, Yogonet, and Travel and Tour World coverage of the 2026 Forbes Travel Guide awards
Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007 — confirmed across multiple independent listings and the property's own marketing copy
AAA Four Diamond Award credential confirmed via Visit Greater Palm Springs and aggregated listings
OpenTable Diners' Choice and Palm Springs Life Best of the Best — confirmed; 2025 Best of the Best readers' poll named it Best Steak in Rancho Mirage
Surfaces strongly on 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage' query — appears alongside The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton) as one of the two named anchors in AI-summarized results

'Only Forbes Four-Star rated restaurant in Coachella Valley' claim is widely repeated in third-party coverage (Casino Life Magazine, Travel and Tour World, Indian Gaming) — though sourced from property PR, it has propagated into the citation web
Operating at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa Rancho Mirage — confirmed
Off-site structured presence via OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs — confirmed
contradicts: Longevity claim of '~19+ years of operation' is understated — the property opened April 6, 2001 (25+ years as of 2026); the Wine-Spectator-since-2007 anchor is real but understates the venue's overall tenure
OpenTable review-count footnote 'exact OpenTable count UNVERIFIED' is now verifiable: Rancho Mirage location shows ~3,509 OpenTable reviews per search index (high volume, consistent with anchor-tier)

Brief asserts strong surfacing on 'date night Rancho Mirage' — independent search shows date-night roundups lead with Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Shame On The Moon, and The Edge Steakhouse; The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente did NOT appear in the top date-night summary results. Date-night surfacing is weaker than claimed.

Sentiment characterization 'recurring caveat about casino-floor noise bleeding into ambiance' — plausible and consistent with a 3.9 Yelp average against Forbes-tier credentials, but individual review-text themes were not directly inspected (Yelp page fetches blocked)

Forbes Travel Guide 'multi-year' Four-Star tenure — search results variably cite 'six consecutive years' and 'seventh consecutive year'; the precise streak length is internally inconsistent across sources
input.notes: Core credential stack (Forbes 2026 Four-Star, Wine Spectator since 2007, AAA Four Diamond, OpenTable Diners' Choice, Palm Springs Life Best of the Best, "only Forbes Four-Star in Coachella Valley") is well-corroborated across multiple independent trade-press and tourism-board sources — assessment holds. Two real corrections: (1) longevity is materially understated (property opened April 2001, ~25 years, not 19+); (2) date-night query surfacing is weaker than claimed — Wally's Desert Turtle and Willie's Modern Fare dominate that lens in Rancho Mirage. Structured-data claim remains unverifiable due to WAF block; the brief's own UNVERIFIED hedge is correct. Visibility-gap score of 3 and anchor-reference tier are defensible: strong anchor on steakhouse/Forbes/Rancho Mirage queries, weaker on broader generalist and date-night queries, exactly as the brief states (modulo the date-night overstatement).
